behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the CLI↔git interaction: rename detection depends on real git diff/status semantics (often via subprocess and index/worktree state). A mock parser can miss real rename classification behavior and would hide failures where git emits rename metadata differently. Higher levels rejected: behavior has no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by the behavior’s deterministic git-classification scope.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline, perform a rename, run the CLI as a real process, and assert report shows the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is tied to real git binary diff output and process behavior (encoding/patch format/exit handling). In-process fakes often won’t reproduce binary marker/output edge cases, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Higher levels rejected: no model-quality judgment or non-deterministic model output; no chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., image), run the CLI process, assert zero crash and binary file is counted in the appropriate category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure occurs at startup against real repository state (`HEAD` unborn, git return codes/messages), which commonly surfaces through subprocess/CLI boundary. A mocked repo API can miss actual no-commit behavior. Higher levels rejected: behavior is deterministic repository-state handling, not AI output or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real empty git repo with no commits, execute the CLI process, and assert successful exit with an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real git history traversal and CLI output, crossing filesystem + git process boundaries. Mock history objects can pass while real revision/range semantics fail. Higher levels rejected: no LLM/ML content-quality dependency and no multi-agent sequencing in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to counts computed from authoritative git commands over the same range.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are notoriously boundary-sensitive and often exposed via subprocess calls. Fakes can hide discrepancies in how staged/unstaged diffs are produced. Higher levels rejected: behavior concerns deterministic git state comparison, with no AI/ML judgment and no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent changes once as staged and once as unstaged, run the CLI process in both states, and assert byte-identical structured output.
